# **Document Manager \- Engineering Development Plan**

| Executive summary Engineering should be implemented as an additive extension of the existing Document Manager platform. The MVP should reuse platform-level capabilities, keep Computo Metrico business logic isolated, and add a dedicated Engineering ingestion, indexing, retrieval and task layer. |
| :---- |

* Reuse existing platform services: FastAPI structure, auth/session, Azure clients, Blob/Queue, CosmosDB access, worker/job pattern, LLM client, audit logging and deployment model.  
* Do not reuse Computo Metrico PDF extraction/classification as Engineering business logic. Those modules are Computo-specific and should be isolated under a computo\_metrico package.  
* Use CosmosDB vector search plus metadata filtering for the MVP RAG/retrieval layer. A separate semantic search service can remain a future improvement, not an MVP dependency.  
* Build Engineering use cases incrementally: foundation \-\> Search API \-\> citation-ready RAG \-\> Fuzzy Search \-\> Norme Check \-\> Elaborati Check \-\> UI/evaluation.

# **1\. MVP boundary**

The Engineering module is intended to help engineering teams search, validate and audit technical project repositories. It should behave as a reliable evidence and citation system first, and as a conversational assistant second.

| Area | MVP decision |
| :---- | :---- |
| Supported files | Parse and retrieve content from PDF, DOCX and XLSX only. Other engineering formats such as DWG/CAD are out of scope for the MVP. |
| Core outputs | Answer or task result with source file, page/sheet, last modified date and contextual snippet. |
| Search scope | Global repository search or discipline/folder scoped search. |
| Main use cases | Citation-ready RAG, Fuzzy Search, Norme cross-reference check and Elaborati completeness check. |
| Non-goals | Generic chatbot behavior, unsupported file parsing, disruptive Computo Metrico refactor. |

# **2\. Reuse boundary and codebase organization**

| Development principle Reuse platform capabilities, not domain-specific Computo Metrico business extraction logic. |
| :---- |

The current codebase already contains reusable platform components. Engineering should build on these shared services while keeping Computo Metrico and Engineering as separate domain packages.

| Existing component | Reuse in Engineering | Reason |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| FastAPI app, routers and schemas | Yes | Add Engineering endpoints without changing existing Computo Metrico routes. |
| Auth, session and role services | Yes | Use the same access and session model for Engineering pages and APIs. |
| Azure client/config layer | Yes | Reuse centralized access to CosmosDB, Blob Storage, Queue and secrets/configuration. |
| Blob, Queue, Job and Worker services | Yes | Run ingestion, sync, parsing, embedding and task execution as asynchronous jobs. |
| Audit/logging service | Yes | Record sync lifecycle, parser status, task execution, query logs and confidence scores. |
| LLM client / Gemini adapter | Yes | Use for grounded answer generation and limited parser fallback where needed. |
| EDH connectors/indexer | Reuse or generalize carefully | Useful as a reference for SharePoint/Azure Files connectors and Cosmos vector indexing patterns. |
| Computo PDF extraction / classification | No, not as Engineering code | Contains Computo-specific prompts, schemas, extraction flow, classification taxonomy and XLSX output logic. |

Proposed package structure:

| src/document\_manager/  computo\_metrico/    classification/    pdf\_extraction/    pipeline.py    ...  engineering/    connectors/    manifest/    parsers/    chunking/    indexing/    retrieval/    rag/    tasks/      fuzzy\_search/      norme\_check/      elaborati\_check/    schemas/    services/  shared/                 \# only if a utility is truly domain-independent    text/    files/    metadata/ |
| :---- |

* The package cleanup should be non-disruptive: move Computo-only modules under computo\_metrico, update imports, and keep temporary compatibility wrappers until existing routes/workers/tests are migrated.  
* Engineering development should be additive: new modules live under document\_manager/engineering and reuse shared app-level services instead of modifying Computo-specific flows.  
* Only utilities that are truly domain-independent should be moved to shared; Computo prompts, taxonomies, schemas and XLSX exporters should remain inside computo\_metrico.

# **3\. Target architecture**

The Engineering architecture adds an ingestion, index and retrieval layer on top of the existing Document Manager cloud components. RAG and automated tasks share the same manifest, chunks, metadata and vector search layer.

![][image1]

| Step | Data flow |
| :---- | :---- |
| 1\. Repository scan | Connector scans local folder, SharePoint or Azure Files and creates/updates the Engineering file manifest. |
| 2\. Change detection | Sync service detects added, modified, deleted, moved and renamed files using path, hash, size and last modified metadata. |
| 3\. ETL | Engineering parsers extract text from PDF/DOCX/XLSX with page or sheet metadata and parser status. |
| 4\. Chunking/indexing | Chunks are embedded and stored in CosmosDB together with citation metadata and active/latest version flags. |
| 5\. Retrieval/RAG | Search API retrieves top evidence from CosmosDB vector search, applies scope filtering, and sends only retrieved context to the LLM. |
| 6\. Automated tasks | Fuzzy, Norme and Elaborati modules reuse the same manifest, normalization, retrieval, citation and logging services. |

# **4\. CosmosDB vector search approach**

For the MVP, CosmosDB vector search is sufficient for Engineering RAG and task retrieval. This avoids adding a separate search service at the beginning and keeps the architecture aligned with the current platform deployment.

| CosmosDB collection / logical container | Content | Used by |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| engineering\_files | File manifest, path, discipline, source connector, hash, size, last modified date, parser status, active flag. | Sync, Elaborati, UI status, audit. |
| engineering\_document\_versions | Version records for modified files and lifecycle history. | Incremental sync, audit, rollback/debug. |
| engineering\_chunks\_vectors | Chunk text, embedding vector, file id, version id, page/sheet, snippet, folder/discipline, active/latest flag. | Search API, RAG, Fuzzy, Norme. |
| engineering\_sync\_jobs | Sync and ETL job status, errors, timestamps and retry metadata. | Operations and monitoring. |
| engineering\_query\_logs | User/session id, selected discipline/domain, raw query, top results, confidence scores and task output ids. | Telemetry, threshold tuning and evaluation. |

* Retrieval should support discipline/folder metadata filtering before or during vector search.  
* Citations must come from indexed metadata, not from LLM-generated text.  
* If the retrieved evidence is insufficient, the RAG assistant must explicitly return a not-found response.  
* Fuzzy behavior should be implemented in the application layer through normalization, string similarity and configurable thresholds; vector retrieval can help find semantically close candidates.

# **5\. Use case dependency model**

The Engineering use cases should build on top of each other. The first use case creates reusable retrieval and scoring capabilities; later use cases add narrower business checks.

| Layer / use case | Builds | Reused by later work |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Shared foundation | Connector, file manifest, sync lifecycle, parser status, Engineering parsers, chunk schema, embedding, CosmosDB vector index, citation formatter and query logger. | All use cases. |
| Use Case 1 \- Fuzzy Search | Query normalization, scope filtering, candidate retrieval, configurable threshold, confidence score, contextual snippets and query telemetry. | Norme Check reuses search/scoring/logging. |
| Use Case 2 \- Norme Check | Fuzzy search for standard references, standard-name normalization, version/format difference flag, Norme library presence check. | Can reuse manifest and search foundation; adds standard-specific logic only. |
| Use Case 3 \- Elaborati Check | Checklist parser, expected deliverables extraction, physical file scan, name matching, Present/Absent matrix and last modified date. | Reuses manifest, connector and normalization; does not require RAG first. |

# **6\. Additive development milestones**

Each milestone should deliver a small, testable increment and avoid breaking the existing Computo Metrico functionality.

| Milestone | Goal | Atomic tasks | Deliverable |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| M0 \- Architecture and package cleanup | Clarify boundaries before feature development. | Create computo\_metrico and engineering packages; move Computo-only modules; update imports; keep compatibility wrappers; run existing Computo smoke tests. | Clean code structure with no behavior regression. |
| M1 \- Engineering connector and manifest | Create the file inventory foundation. | Implement DocumentSourceConnector interface; local-folder connector first; manifest schema; parser status fields; basic scan API/job. | Repository inventory with path, discipline, last modified, hash and status. |
| M2 \- Incremental sync lifecycle | Keep the active index aligned with repository changes. | Detect added/modified/deleted/moved files; active/latest flags; soft deletion; sync logs and retry behavior. | Manual Sync Now and scheduled sync support. |
| M3 \- Engineering ETL and chunking | Create citation-ready chunks. | Implement Engineering PDF/DOCX/XLSX parsers; page/sheet metadata; chunking rules; parser error reporting. | Chunks ready for indexing with file/page/sheet metadata. |
| M4 \- CosmosDB vector index and Search API | Validate retrieval before RAG. | Embedding generation; Cosmos vector insert/update; discipline/folder filter; top-k Search API; result snippets. | /v1/engineering/search returning evidence and citations. |
| M5 \- Citation-ready RAG | Generate grounded answers only from retrieved evidence. | Context builder; prompt template; LLM call; citation formatter; not-found behavior; response schema. | /v1/engineering/rag with source file, page/sheet and last modified date. |
| M6 \- Use Case 1: Fuzzy Search | First automated task on top of search. | Normalization; configurable threshold; similarity/confidence score; Potential Matches output; query logging. | Fuzzy results table with snippets and scores. |
| M7 \- Use Case 2: Norme Check | Build on Fuzzy Search. | Standard normalization; version/format mismatch flag; Norme library folder scan; Present/Absent status. | Norme citation list, differences alert and library presence flag. |
| M8 \- Use Case 3: Elaborati Check | Completeness audit using manifest. | Checklist parser; expected vs actual matching; last modified date; Present/Absent matrix export. | Deliverables completeness matrix. |
| M9 \- UI, evaluation and pilot hardening | Prepare for pilot use. | Engineering UI pages; permissions; logs; golden dataset; retrieval top-5 evaluation; operational dashboard. | Pilot-ready Engineering module. |

# **7\. Acceptance criteria**

| Area | Minimum acceptance criterion |
| :---- | :---- |
| Non-disruptive migration | Existing Computo Metrico conversion/classification workflows continue to pass smoke tests after package cleanup. |
| Parsing | Supported PDF/DOCX/XLSX files are parsed with page or sheet metadata; failures are visible and auditable. |
| Retrieval | Golden dataset expected documents/pages appear in Top 5 results for the selected discipline/domain. |
| Citation | Every factual answer includes source file, page/sheet and last modified date from indexed metadata. |
| RAG safety | If the answer is not present in the selected evidence, the assistant returns not found instead of guessing. |
| Dynamic sync | Added, modified, deleted and renamed/moved files are reflected in active search results. |
| Task telemetry | Fuzzy and Norme executions log raw query, scope, returned documents/pages and confidence scores for tuning. |

# **8\. Immediate next steps**

* Confirm the target package structure and create the non-disruptive computo\_metrico / engineering split.  
* Document the existing deployed cloud components and map them to Engineering reuse points.  
* Implement the Engineering connector interface and local-folder manifest as the first technical increment.  
* Define the CosmosDB chunk/vector schema and citation metadata contract before building RAG.  
* Prepare a small golden dataset for Search API and Fuzzy Search evaluation.
